findings.suggestion: Before extracting, iterate zf.infolist() and enforce limits: reject if any info.file_size exceeds a per-file cap, if sum(file_size) exceeds a total cap, if len(infolist) exceeds a member cap, or if file_size/compress_size exceeds a ratio threshold. Extract members individually with a bounded read loop (read fixed-size chunks and abort past a running byte budget) rather than extractall. Also document that archive_path may be untrusted.
findings.summary: source.py:8-9 calls zf.extractall(dest) directly with no cap on per-member uncompressed size, total extracted bytes, member count, or compression ratio. As the comment at lines 6-7 acknowledges, a small crafted archive (zip bomb) can decompress to arbitrarily large output, exhausting disk and memory (DoS). The function name/signature (extract_archive, line 5) advertises no trust precondition, so with 0 current callers the next integration is likely to feed it untrusted input and inherit the vulnerability.
findings.title: Zip bomb: extract_archive decompresses without size/ratio/count limits

summary:
Risk-tiered review (trivial) completed.

Single new file introducing extract_archive, which extracts a zip with zipfile.extractall and no decompression limits — a genuine zip-bomb / resource-exhaustion vulnerability that the code's own comment acknowledges. Stated intent and code agree (no intent/code contradiction), but the function relies on an unenforced and unadvertised 'archive is trusted' invariant, which is the material assumption risk for follow-up work. Path traversal (Zip Slip) is NOT present because CPython's extractall sanitizes member paths — deliberately not reported to avoid a false finding.

Single newly-added function extract_archive extracts a zip with zipfile.extractall and no size/ratio guards — a real decompression-bomb DoS (CWE-409), matching the case annotation. Path traversal (Zip Slip) is largely mitigated by modern extractall's own sanitization, so it is not reported as a separate finding. No callers exist yet (0 blast radius), but any use on untrusted input is unsafe.
